text.title: Ai Pairing Decision Tree Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Ai Pairing Decision Tree application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Score every task across all six axes — reversibility, blast radius, novelty, test coverage, regulatory exposure, fatigue. A missing axis defaults to worst-case (3).
rule.rationale: Skipping an axis is the #1 source of mis-routing. Defaulting to 3 enforces conservative bias when an axis is unclear.

rule.statement: If fatigue ≥ 2 (late night, >6h coding, headache) the mode downgrades by one step regardless of total score.
rule.rationale: Tired solo devs accept AI suggestions without review. The fatigue veto forces a downshift to compensate.

rule.statement: Sum 0-3 → AGENT-DRIVES; 4-7 → AGENT-DRAFTS; 8-12 → AGENT-ASSISTS; 13-18 → HUMAN-ONLY. Never invent intermediate modes.
rule.rationale: Free-form mode names create drift. Four discrete modes keep the rubric auditable per PR.

rule.statement: Log explicit override-trigger conditions (test fails, type error, lint warn, runtime error, regression in golden output) BEFORE handing control to the agent.
rule.rationale: Without pre-declared triggers, the agent keeps trying patches past the point of useful work.

rule.statement: Mode + axis scores + override-triggers MUST be pasted into the PR description. No silent agent-drives.
rule.rationale: Three months later you cannot reconstruct WHY a 200-line agent-driven change passed without review.
